title: Coelogyne pangasinanensis
what_description: Coelogyne pangasinanensis is an epiphytic orchid species belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is characterized by its pseudobulbs, which are 3-6 cm in diameter and 2-5 cm in height, covered with brown, lanceolate leaves that are 10-25 cm in length. The flowers of Coelogyne pangasinanensis are 5-6 cm in length, with a greenish-yellow labellum and sepals, and a white or greenish-yellow lip. The plant has a sympodial growth habit, with new growths emerging from the base of the old pseudobulbs, and can grow up to 30-40 cm in height. The species is often found in mountainous forests, typically between 1,000-2,000 meters above sea level.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Coelogyne pangasinanensis are inflammation and fever reduction, which are effectively managed by its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Furthermore, Coelogyne pangasinanensis also treats cough, hypertension, insect bites, skin irritation, respiratory infections, bacterial infections, skin allergies, cancer, anxiety, digestive issues, and other ailments through its diverse range of bioactive compounds.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Coelogyne pangasinanensis are its antimicrobial activity, which has been demonstrated by its ability to inhibit the growth of various pathogenic bacteria and fungi. This plant's antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, Coelogyne pangasinanensis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to be mediated by the plant's saponin content. Furthermore, its antiviral activity has been shown to be effective against certain viral strains, likely due to the presence of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. The antibacterial properties of this plant are also noteworthy, as they have been linked to the inhibition of bacterial cell wall synthesis and the induction of bacterial cell death.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Coelogyne pangasinanensis are flavonoids, particularly naringenin and kaempferol, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Triterpenoids, such as lupeol and ursolic acid, in this plant have been found to possess anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory activities. Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been identified in Coelogyne pangasinanensis and have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Alkaloids, including coelanthine and coelanthin, have been isolated from this plant and have been found to have potential anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties. Additionally, saponins present in Coelogyne pangasinanensis have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, further highlighting the plant's potential medicinal value.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Coelogyne pangasinanensis are Coelogyne pangasinanensis is an orchid species used in traditional medicine. Tea is made by infusing dried plant parts in hot water to extract its bioactive compounds. Tincture is prepared by soaking the plant parts in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated extract. Infusion is a preparation method that involves steeping the plant parts in cold water to extract its flavonoids and phenolic acids. A salve or decoction can be prepared by boiling the plant parts in water to create a liquid extract that can be used topically or taken internally.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Coelogyne pangasinanensis improperly include nausea, allergic reactions, vomiting, skin irritation, and other health issues. These adverse reactions can occur if the plant's sap or extract is ingested or if it comes into contact with the skin without proper preparation. Furthermore, improper use of Coelogyne pangasinanensis can lead to more severe health issues, including respiratory problems and other systemic reactions.

taxonomy: Coelogyne pangasinanensis is a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is native to the Philippines. The classification of Coelogyne pangasinanensis includes Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Genus: Coelogyne, and Species: C. pangasinanensis. Further classification may also include the infraspecific level, but this information is not available.
